decision: Keep analytics substitutions typed and default-normalized
reason: Replacing interface{}-backed metrics with typed DTOs and collapsing nil collaborator overrides to default implementations prevents callers from depending on concrete runtime type assertions or nil-sensitive service/repository state.
impact: Analytics model accessors are now substitution-safe, and injected repository/service strategies behave consistently across constructors, options, and tests.

decision: Keep analytics LSP cleanup centered on default-safe seams and reusable typed helpers
reason: The scoped analytics work already refactored tests and typed metric models. The remaining LSP-sensitive changes are the service/repository option seams and helper-level behavior that let alternate collaborators be substituted without changing calling code or violating backend boundaries.
impact: Analytics service/repository defaults, typed metric DTO accessors, and the scoped analytics tests now align around stable substitution-friendly seams while preserving existing package boundaries.
